source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design and analyze a 6U small-satellite equipment box per NASA-HDBK-7005 (2001) random-vibration, sine, and shock qualification, delivered as a multi-part assembly because the flange-constrained first-mode, the random-response 3-sigma stress, the sine and shock response, and the quasi-static enveloping all depend on the coupled dynamic behavior of the Al 6061 enclosure, the three internal PCBs, the M3 standoffs, and the 6 M4 mounting fasteners on the flange. Qualification envelopes come from the GEVS / NASA-HDBK-7005 reference (GSFC-STD-7000) for component-level random vibration, sine, and separation-shock SRS.

Assembly layout. (A) Enclosure: 200 mm x 150 mm x 70 mm milled Al 6061-T6 box, nominal 2 mm wall (1.5-3.0 mm range), flange perimeter with 6 M4 fastener holes on a 180 mm x 130 mm pattern. (B) PCBs (x3): 180 mm x 130 mm x 1.6 mm FR4, each 150 g, mounted at Z = 30, 45, 60 mm on 4 M3 standoffs per board. (C) M3 standoffs (x12): short Al 6061 posts supporting PCBs off the enclosure floor. (D) M4 mounting fasteners (x6): A286 or Ti fasteners clamping the flange to the spacecraft panel.

Material. Enclosure Al 6061-T6 (E = 68.9 GPa, nu = 0.33, rho = 2700 kg/m^3, Fty = 276 MPa, Ftu = 310 MPa). PCBs FR4 (E = 22 GPa in-plane, nu = 0.18, rho = 1850 kg/m^3); may be treated as lumped masses coupled via RBE2 to the standoffs.

Qualification environment (NASA-HDBK-7005 / GEVS). Random PSD: +6 dB/oct 20-50 Hz (0.013 -> 0.08 g^2/Hz), plateau 0.08 g^2/Hz 50-800 Hz, -6 dB/oct 800-2000 Hz (0.08 -> 0.013 g^2/Hz), overall 14.1 Grms, 2 min/axis. Sine: 0.5 inch DA below 7 Hz, 12.5 g peak 7-100 Hz at 2 oct/min. Shock SRS: 100 Hz = 100 g, 1000 Hz = 2000 g, plateau to 10 kHz, Q = 10.

Load cases. LC1: random vibration per PSD, 3 axes individually. LC2: sine 12.5 g swept 7-100 Hz, 3 axes. LC3: shock SRS each axis. LC4: quasi-static enveloping 30 g each axis simultaneously.

Pass/fail criteria (all on assembled box with PCBs as internal mass). (R1) Flange-constrained first natural frequency >= 140 Hz (decoupling gate). (R2) Max 3-sigma random response stress in enclosure walls under LC1 <= Ftu/1.4 = 221.4 MPa. (R3) Max 3-sigma random response stress in fastener ligament under LC1 <= Fty/1.25 = 220.8 MPa. (R4) Sine LC2 peak stress <= 220.8 MPa. (R5) Shock LC3 SRS-response max stress <= 221.4 MPa. (R6) LC4 30 g quasi-static max stress <= 220.8 MPa. (R7) Empty enclosure mass <= 800 g.

Assembly policy. The first natural frequency and all response stresses depend on the enclosure wall stiffness in combination with the internal 450 g of PCB mass, the standoff stiffness, and the flange fastener boundary. PCB-alone or enclosure-alone modal extraction gives meaningless frequencies. Fastener-ligament stress (R3) exists only in the flange + fastener combined geometry.

Solver and analysis expectations. Constrained modal extraction (R1), modal-superposition PSD random response (R2, R3), harmonic sine response (R4), SRS response (R5), linear static (R6). TET10 solids for enclosure walls at 2 mm mesh; PCBs as lumped masses via RBE2 to standoffs; fasteners as CBUSH or beam couplings. Buckling, thermal, fluid, ionizing-radiation analyses NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of enclosure + PCB stack, empty mass in g, first 10 modes, 3-sigma random stress per axis for LC1, sine peak stress, shock response stress, and LC4 quasi-static max stress.
short_prompt:
Design a 6U small-satellite equipment box per NASA-HDBK-7005 (2001), delivered as a multi-part assembly for random vibration, sine, and shock qualification (GSFC-STD-7000 / GEVS reference). Assembly: a milled Al 6061-T6 enclosure (200 x 150 x 70 mm, nominal 2 mm wall, flange with 6 M4 holes on a 180 x 130 mm pattern) + three 180 x 130 x 1.6 mm FR4 PCBs (150 g each at Z = 30, 45, 60 mm on 4 M3 standoffs) + twelve M3 Al 6061 standoffs + six A286/Ti M4 mounting fasteners. Al 6061-T6: E = 68.9 GPa, nu = 0.33, rho = 2700 kg/m^3, Fty = 276 MPa, Ftu = 310 MPa. FR4 E = 22 GPa, nu = 0.18, rho = 1850 kg/m^3.

Qualification environment (NASA-HDBK-7005 / GEVS). Random PSD: +6 dB/oct 20-50 Hz (0.013 -> 0.08 g^2/Hz), plateau 0.08 g^2/Hz 50-800 Hz, -6 dB/oct 800-2000 Hz (0.08 -> 0.013 g^2/Hz), overall 14.1 Grms, 2 min/axis. Sine: 0.5 inch DA below 7 Hz, 12.5 g peak 7-100 Hz at 2 oct/min. Shock SRS: 100 Hz = 100 g, 1000 Hz = 2000 g, plateau to 10 kHz, Q = 10.

Load cases: LC1 random per PSD, 3 axes; LC2 sine 12.5 g 7-100 Hz; LC3 shock SRS each axis; LC4 quasi-static 30 g each axis simultaneously.

Pass limits: first mode >= 140 Hz; LC1 3-sigma wall stress <= Ftu/1.4 = 221.4 MPa; fastener-ligament 3-sigma <= Fty/1.25 = 220.8 MPa; LC2 peak <= 220.8 MPa; LC3 <= 221.4 MPa; LC4 <= 220.8 MPa; empty enclosure mass <= 800 g.

prompt.title: Satellite equipment box random/sine/shock qualification as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: 200x150x70 mm Al 6061 enclosure + 3x150g FR4 PCBs + M3 standoffs + 6 M4 flange fasteners; GEVS 14.1 Grms random, 12.5 g sine, separation-shock SRS; fn >= 140 Hz on the assembled box.

parts.name: enclosure
parts.role: milled Al 6061-T6 box hosting the PCB stack and bolted to the spacecraft panel at the flange

parts.name: pcb
parts.role: FR4 PCB internal card providing 150 g internal mass at a Z position

parts.name: m3_standoff
parts.role: Al standoff supporting a PCB off the enclosure floor

parts.name: m4_flange_fastener
parts.role: flange bolt clamping enclosure to spacecraft panel

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Decoupling from PSD plateau 50-800 Hz per NASA-HDBK-7005 guidance

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: TET10 / shell walls with lumped PCBs

interfaces.load_path: PCB inertia reaction through standoff to enclosure wall
PCB dynamic response transferred to standoff and then enclosure
all base excitation enters through the 6 flange fasteners

fem_check_policy.rationale: First natural frequency (R1) and all random/sine/shock response stresses (R2-R5) depend on the combined enclosure walls + 450 g internal PCB mass + standoff stiffness + flange fastener boundary. Fastener-ligament stress exists only in the flange+fastener combined geometry.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: NASA-HDBK-7005 qualification is performed at the component (box+internals) level; modal decoupling and response stress are assembly-scope properties.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Environment is vibration/shock; thermal out of scope.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: EEE parts derating per component-level vibration stress
Acoustic response (fairing acoustic field) beyond random-vib envelope

eval_coverage: PSD random response 3-sigma stress on assembled box
sine + shock SRS with internal PCB mass coupling
flange-constrained modal decoupling through fastener boundary
GEVS/NASA-HDBK-7005 full envelope on assembled geometry
